creator: ohartmann@walstatt.org
text:
Port x11-toolkits/swt-devel breaks compiling with the following error due to a patcheset error:


 swt-devel is using firefox for gecko support, but you can
 change that by defining WITH_GECKO to the following values:

   mozilla 
   firefox 
   seamonkey 

===>  Vulnerability check disabled, database not found
===>  Found saved configuration for swt-devel-3.3M6_1
=> swt-3.3-gtk-linux-x86_64.zip doesn't seem to exist in /usr/ports/distfiles/.
=> Attempting to fetch from http://download.eclipse.org/eclipse/downloads/drops/R-3.3-200706251500/.
swt-3.3-gtk-linux-x86_64.zip                  100% of 4513 kB  290 kBps 00m00s
===>  Extracting for swt-devel-3.3_1,1
=> MD5 Checksum OK for swt-3.3-gtk-linux-x86_64.zip.
=> SHA256 Checksum OK for swt-3.3-gtk-linux-x86_64.zip.
===>   swt-devel-3.3_1,1 depends on executable: unzip - found
===>  Patching for swt-devel-3.3_1,1
===>  Applying FreeBSD patches for swt-devel-3.3_1,1
1 out of 1 hunks failed--saving rejects to ./os.c.rej
=> Patch patch-os.c failed to apply cleanly.
=> Patch(es) patch-OS.java patch-build.sh patch-build.xml patch-make_freebsd.mak applied cleanly.
*** Error code 1

Stop in /usr/ports/x11-toolkits/swt-devel.
*** Error code 1

Stop in /usr/ports/x11-toolkits/swt-devel.

How-To-Repeat: Try building x11-toolkits/swt-devel on FreeBSD 7.0-CURRENT/AMD64
